member.office_address: 566 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-2201

form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
This is the FY27 Appropriations request form for programmatic (budgetary legislative proposal) and language (policy legislative proposal) requests for the office of Congressman Jack Bergman.
If you would like to submit for Community Project Funding (state or local government or eligible non-profit recipient) you will need to contact Kenny Keller (kenneth.keller@mail.house.gov) for the appropriate form
DUE 3/6. Date is subject to change and will be updated on the form

sections.description: Information about the individual and organization submitting the request.
General overview of the appropriations request.

fields.label: Title of Request
fields.rationale: Using the 'Short Title' provided in the strategic context for the NASA Science account.

fields.label: If you are submitting multiple requests, what is the priority of this request? (EX: 1 of 3)
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific prioritization question that cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: Does this request have any specific impact on Michigan's First district or the State of Michigan? If so, please describe
fields.draft_value:
* Over $7.5M in recent NASA Science obligations in MI-01, including growing annual investments of $3.3M in FY2025.
* Michigan Technological University (Houghton) leads critical research using satellite data for wildfire risk modeling and Great Lakes wetland tracking.
* Funding supports high-tech jobs and STEM workforce development at local institutions like Northern Wings Repair and MTU.
* Local research directly impacts regional safety and policy, including wildfire smoke prediction and waterfowl habitat protection.
fields.rationale: Cites specific district spending totals ($7.5M) and top recipients (MTU, Northern Wings) with specific grant-funded research topics relevant to MI-01.

fields.label: Which Subcommittee does this request fall under?
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is funded under the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee.

fields.label: What amount was requested for this program in the President's FY23 and FY24 Budget Request?
fields.draft_value: FY23 Request: $7.988 billion; FY24 Request: $8.261 billion
fields.rationale: Providing the exact budget request figures from the SMD Topline Funding History.

fields.label: What is the FY23 enacted appropriations level for this line item/program?
fields.rationale: Providing the exact FY23 Enacted amount from the SMD Topline Funding History.

fields.label: Does this budgetary proposal include any changes to direct (mandatory) spending?
fields.rationale: NASA Science funding is discretionary spending, not mandatory.

fields.label: Please include any further description or summary of the program below:
fields.draft_value:
* NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) manages a robust portfolio of over 100 space missions exploring the Earth, Sun, Solar System, and universe.
* Requesting $9 billion to restore stability following FY2026 disruptions and ensure continued U.S. leadership in the face of international competition.
* SMD's work provides essential data for national needs, from weather forecasting and wildfire tracking to planetary defense and deep space exploration.
* Investments utilize the Decadal Surveys to ensure research priorities are based on nonpartisan, peer-reviewed scientific consensus.
fields.rationale: Provides a summary of SMD's scope and the specific $9B funding request, incorporating the context of FY2026 disruptions and the use of Decadal Surveys.

fields.label: Has this language been included in previous fiscal year House-passed, Senate-passed, or enacted appropriations bills?
fields.rationale: The Science account is a standard, existing part of the NASA appropriations structure included in prior enacted bills.

fields.label: Proposal description and justification:
fields.draft_value:
* Space science is a fundamental public responsibility that advances U.S. security and economic competitiveness beyond the scope of private industry.
* Sustained investment is critical as China rapidly expands its space science ambitions, including lunar sample returns and Mars exploration.
* Every $1 invested in NASA returns approximately $3 to the economy, supporting a specialized workforce across more than 75% of congressional districts.
* This $9 billion request reverses harmful cuts that previously led to the loss of thousands of highly skilled aerospace contractors and civil servants.
fields.rationale: Draws on strategic talking points regarding competition with China, economic return on investment, and the necessity of restoring the workforce after prior cuts.

fields.label: Is this policy legislative proposal for bill language or report language?
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request, not a request for bill or report language. These fields are typically mutually exclusive.

fields.label: Proposed bill language or report language:
fields.draft_value: Science: For necessary expenses, not otherwise provided for, in the conduct and support of aerospace and aeronautical research and development activities, including research, development, operations, support, and services; $9,000,000,000.
fields.rationale: Provides standard appropriations bill language format incorporating the requested $9 billion figure for the Science account.

top_grants.description: Informing human risk perception of and response to wildland fire smoke using new satellite derived extreme smoke event data
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Analyzing satellite smoke data to guide wildfire risk perception.

top_grants.description: e014042- integrating remote sensing and modeling to better understand the vulnerability of boreal-taiga ecosystems to wildfire
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Modeling boreal-taiga ecosystem wildfire risk using remote sensing.

top_grants.description: eo14042 informing wetland policy and management for waterfowl habitat and other ecosystem services using multi-frequency synthetic aperture radar
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Guiding wetland policy for waterfowl habitat protection using radar.

top_grants.description: The goal of the proposed project is to develop and implement an effective and sustainable framework for identifying  mapping  and monitoring Vernal pools in the Great Lakes region. This project will integrate citizen science with a novel multi-sensor remote sensing technique combining canopy penetrating Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) with lidar Digital Elevation Model (DEM) stochastic depression analysis to facilitate Vernal Pool mapping and monitoring. We plan to convene project partners and stakeholders to develop a Great Lakes Vernal Pool Collaborative (GLVPC) and citizen science program for all eight states. Ensuring the involvement of all states in the definition of Vernal pools and standards and protocols for monitoring from the outset is crucial for establishing the glvpc from its inception with vested interests by all. However  the mapping and citizen science program will be conducted in 3 focus states  michigan  wisconsin and new york  due to time and budget constraints. We will establish the Great Lakes Vernal Pool citizen science program using the framework  and tools/resources identified and/or developed during workshops of partners. We will map pvps in focus areas of the 3 states using remote sensing and recruit and train people for the citizen science program to verify and collect Vernal Pool data. Efforts will include engaging partners  refining Digital app field forms (survey 123 and epicollect)  building a glvpc website and webviewer for storing and viewing data  as well as creating resources to recruit  train  and retain program participants. This will establish a network of trained and engaged citizen scientists and partners to implement and sustain successful.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Establishing a collaborative framework for tracking Great Lakes wetlands via citizen science.

top_grants.description: Characterizing hourly fire activity using goes abi and viirs to improve the prediction of smoke impacts and timing
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Predicting wildfire smoke timing and impact using satellite data.

top_grants.description: Sediment plumes and blooms: using Earth observations and modeling to forecast post-fire impacts to reservoir water.quality and quantity.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Forecasting reservoir water quality changes after wildfires.

top_grants.description: This proposal is aimed at a systematic assessment of desertification and its impact on aeolian dust  air quality  and human health in central asia during the past twenty years (2000-2019).
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Assessing desertification's impact on Central Asian air quality and health.

top_grants.description: Validation of NASA pace oci products for the laurentian great lakes
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Verifying NASA ocean color data for Great Lakes environmental monitoring.
